changelog.author: model
changelog.change: 7-step chain rebuild with explicit Step 3 (selected-nodes filter). Per-node TAMs reconciled bottom-up from role library × employment survey (NO scaling factors). Auto Core $650M / FMCG Core $209M / Combined $1.56B. Full provenance (data + sources + method) preserved in tam_summary per new prime directive.

changelog.author: model
changelog.change: Dropped 2028 forward-view automation multiplier (0.85 Auto / 0.90 FMCG). New chain: payroll × eligibility × task AI rate × 50% discount. Auto $388M Core + $386M ecosystem + $86M budget = $860M; FMCG $302M Core + $278M ecosystem + $194M budget = $774M; Combined $1.63B.
Initial data file creation from website JS objects
Upgraded products to top-funded companies with logos, funding, investors
Added india_focus flag to all products. Added India-origin companies: Moglix, Zetwerk, Detect Technologies, Log9, Altizon, Droom, GoMechanic, Increff, Ninjacart, Zaggle, Docketly, Qualitas Tech
Switched logos from Clearbit to Google Favicons (more reliable). Updated Indian public companies with market cap data.
Updated all Indian company funding data. Empty fields replaced with dash. Public companies show market cap.
Added 5-category source structure with node-specific product evidence links
Updated 17 public/acquired company records with market cap and acquisition data globally
TAM harmonized to $1B enterprise TAM. Added tam_summary with full methodology.
Sector revenue reconciliation: Auto $252.4B→$240B (Invest India), FMCG $287.9B→$245B (IBEF 2024). Per 4-agent website audit 2026-04-11.
Knowledge base cleanup: 09_Master corrections field removed (internally inconsistent); see information_source_map.md for details.

Curve + sector table full rebuild: 7 research agents validated FY20/FY25/FY30 per-sector anchors. Option B (gross sector output framing) locked. New sector table: Auto 159/240/363, FMCG 110/189/259 (narrow), ESDM 67/133/313, Chem 190/260/383, Metals 110/140/172, Pharma 35/55/130. Totals: FY20 $671B, FY25 $1.02T, FY30 $1.62T. Chart curve FY18-FY30 rebuilt with COVID dip at FY21 ($610B, -9% vs FY20). FY25 primary-source verified: Auto (Invest India + PIB), ESDM (PIB Oct 2025, Rs11.3Lcr), Chemicals (Invest India), Pharma (IBEF/Bain). Chart x-axis relabeled FY18-FY30. Timeline table column headers shifted from CY to FY. Methodology note rewritten as bottom-up sum-of-sectors.

Rebuild v2: primary-sourced CAGRs (Auto 7.7% Mordor, ESDM 22.5% CareEdge, Chem 8.0% Invest India, Pharma 18.8% Bain-impl, M&M 11.7% GlobalData, FMCG 6.5% IBEF range); Metals rescoped to Metals & Mining ($285B FY25 GlobalData verbatim, includes coal/iron ore); Pharma FY20 anchored to DoP Annual Report 2019-20 ($41B). New totals: FY20 $732B / FY25 $1.2T / FY30 $2.0T. Decimal numbering rule applied: $1.16T->$1.2T, $1.95T->$2.0T. 19 AI product grid URLs deep-linked (TechCrunch/BusinessWire/SEC/PRNewswire). Hover tooltips added on chart dots. Section 02 desc tightened. Hook bracket text reduced.

KB audit: synced 10 stale product lists to match ai_thesis.html. Removed: Scoutbee, Spot AI, Inspectorio, Anyline, Docketly, Benchling, Veeva Systems, Ninjacart, Eversight, People.ai, Xactly, RetailNext. Added: Aha!, Jira Align, ETQ Reliance, Bosch Diagnostics, Servify, SwitchOn, LabWare, SafetyChain, Pricefx, Bizom, FieldAssist, o9 Solutions.

Added node_evidence: per-dimension scoring evidence (Dim 1 AI Tech Maturity / Dim 2 Knowledge Work Density / Dim 3 India Deployment) + score_sources for all 28 nodes. All evidence derived from internal data (products[], i_note, existing node_sources).

Rebuilt sector + node-level TAM using 12_Employment_Data_Auto_FMCG.xlsx (30 Auto + 13 FMCG listed-company compensation survey, 55,441 white-collar employees). New numbers: Auto $671M (was $715M, -6%), FMCG $331M (was $528M, -37%), Combined $1.0B (was $1.2B, -19%). FMCG: career-weighted AI addressability raised from 36.5% → 40% (higher Sales+Marketing+Analytics share per data) and task automation intensity raised 70% → 80% (FMCG AI tooling maturity 3-5 yrs ahead of Auto per McKinsey State of AI 2024).

Node-level TAM rebuilt with first-principles 6-step chain mirroring sector flow (Node KW × avg salary × addressability × automation × 50% discount). Node addressability is now per-node (derived from career-stream mix × published rates) rather than inheriting sector-blended factor. Selected-node sum: Auto $286M / FMCG $238M / combined $524M of $1.0B sector TAM; remainder sits in supporting non-selected workflows.

TAM restricted to Selected scope + Package B ecosystem expansion + forward 2028 automation (no salary uplift — survey is fully-loaded comp). Auto $323M Core + $330M ecosystem = $653M; FMCG $267M Core + $250M ecosystem = $517M; Combined $1.17B.

tam_summary._schema_note: PRIME DIRECTIVE: every number in this object includes a data_source + method of derivation so that a future session can reconstruct it without repeating research. Do NOT remove provenance fields when updating.
tam_summary.methodology: 3-SAM framework (Core + Extended + Ecosystem) summed. Core from BRSR direct parse (45 firms) + density extrapolation. Extended sized bottom-up from ACMA/Screener mid-market firm count (~300 Auto + ~200 FMCG) × per-firm KW averages (~280 Auto / ~325 FMCG) + research-backed 22% Auto / 15% FMCG salary compression + 12pp AI-rate compression. Ecosystem from FADA + ACMA + AICPDF + FICCI channel-workforce census.
tam_summary.key_innovation_2026_04_22: Step 3 = explicit selected-nodes filter (66% Auto / 59% FMCG). Derived BOTTOM-UP from role library × employment survey — NO scaling factors. Per-node TAMs sum exactly to sector Core by construction.

auto.universe_count: ~530 brand-owner firms (130 SAM: ≥₹1000 Cr + ≥1000 KW; 400 Extended SAM: ≥₹100 Cr + ≥200 KW)

kw_derivation.universe_kw: 209,000 — from 17 SAM firms BRSR Section A 20(a) Permanent Employees (D-row) directly parsed: Tata Motors 8931, Maruti 12515, M&M 13807, Hero 4508, Eicher 3162, Bajaj 3055, Ashok 5003, TVS 4129, Bharat Forge 2566, Uno Minda 3998, Endurance 2411, Sundram 2043, Amara Raja 2588, Wheels India 1929, Subros 1127, Minda Corp 1827, Lumax 1405 = 78,283 Core SAM verified. Remaining 400 firms extrapolated via verified KW-to-revenue density ratio: 75 KW per ₹1,000 Cr (SAM) and 302 KW per ₹1,000 Cr (Extended SAM) against ₹16.7L Cr sector revenue (₹10L Cr OEMs + ₹6.73L Cr components).
kw_derivation.selected_nodes_kw: 137,129 — BOTTOM-UP from role library × employment survey. For each of 85 Auto roles in 03_Workflow_Role_Mapping_Sequential.xlsx, look up (department × career-stream) HC weight from 12_Employment_Data_Auto_FMCG.xlsx (49,434 Auto WC employees, 30 listed companies). Sum per workflow block; retain only the 11 selected nodes (Vehicle Program Leadership, Testing & Validation, APQP/PPAP, Supply Chain & Sourcing, Supplier Quality & Planning, Operations Leadership, Dealership Management, Showroom Sales, After-Sales Service, Warranty & Parts, Finance & HR). 65.6% = selected_nodes_kw / universe_kw — the OUTPUT, not a scaling input.

salary_source.detail: 30 listed Auto brand-owners + ancillaries, 49,434 WC employees, ₹12,588 Cr total payroll → $30,679 weighted-mean; cross-checked against Aon India TRS

auto.eligibility_source: 81.7% = 100% - (5.3% CXO carve-out + 13% hyper-tech R&D carve-out). CXO from BRSR Principle 5 E3 pay-band data across 9 Auto filings + Section 197 ≥₹1 Cr disclosures. Hyper-tech R&D from McKinsey MGI GenAI role taxonomy (bench engineering / NVH / homologation judged near-100% AI-ineligible for half the Engineering & Science headcount).
auto.ai_rate_source: 37.8% = payroll-weighted task AI rate across 161-role library, 9 career-stream buckets. Per-bucket rates from: WEF Future of Jobs 2025 (https://reports.weforum.org/docs/WEF_Future_of_Jobs_Report_2025.pdf), McKinsey MGI GenAI 2023, Frey-Osborne 2013 (702-occupation automation probability), OECD Employment Outlook 2023. Bucket rates: Clerical 77%, CS 62%, Middle Mgmt 42%, Technician 47%, Sales-field 37%, Professional 30%, Senior Mgmt 20%, Executive 7.5%.
auto.discount_source: 50% = a16z '$20K AI vs $40K labor' heuristic; Bessemer BVP AI pricing playbook documents 30-60% of FTE-cost as early-stage enterprise AI pricing.

sources: FADA Oct 2025 release: 15K dealer members, 30K outlets, 5M total employment (https://fada.in)
ACMA careers page: 1.5M direct workforce, 830 members (https://www.acma.in/careers.php)
Autopunditz dealership structure: ~25 WC per 3S outlet (https://www.autopunditz.com/post/all-about-automobile-dealerships-part-2)
Salary benchmarks: Naukri/Glassdoor/PayScale — Car Sales Exec ₹2L, Service Tech ₹2-8L, Dealer GM ₹21L, Bharat Forge engineer ₹5L, Motherson engineer ₹4.9-8.7L

universe_sources.method: Reckitt India, Mondelez India, PepsiCo India, Coca-Cola India, L'Oreal India, Perfetti, Kimberly-Clark, J&J (9 verified)

kw_derivation.universe_kw: 169,000 — from 21 SAM firms BRSR / MCA-Tofler parsed: HUL 7215, ITC FMCG-only 6000 (derived from FY25 restated ex-Hotels 10,608), Nestle 3980, Britannia 2483, Dabur 4023, Marico 1772, GCPL 1498, Colgate 1500, TCP 2500, Emami 1500, Varun 3200, USL 1402, UBL 1364, Radico 1194, Patanjali 4197, Honasa 923, Adani Wilmar 2772, Bikaji 1363, Hatsun 1857, Avanti 743 + 9 MNC subs + Amul = 57,963 Core SAM verified. Remaining 140 firms extrapolated via 122 KW/₹1000 Cr (SAM) and 309 KW/₹1000 Cr (Extended SAM) against ₹9.1L Cr sector revenue.
kw_derivation.selected_nodes_kw: 98,850 — BOTTOM-UP from role library × employment survey. For each of 83 FMCG roles × (department × career-stream) HC weight from 6,007 FMCG WC employees across 13 companies. Sum per workflow block; retain only the 17 selected nodes. 58.5% = selected_nodes_kw / universe_kw — OUTPUT, not scaling input.

fmcg.salary_source: 12_Employment_Data_Auto_FMCG.xlsx — 13 listed FMCG majors, 6,007 WC employees, ₹721 Cr payroll → $14,469 weighted-mean
fmcg.eligibility_source: 74.6% = 100% - (6.4% CXO + 19% formulation/sensory/brand R&D). Higher R&D carve-out than Auto because FMCG R&D is lab-bench-intensive — AI cannot taste, smell, or run sensory panels.
fmcg.ai_rate_source: 39.1% = same per-bucket rates as Auto, reweighted by FMCG payroll (more sales + clerical, marginally higher rate)

sources: HUL Annual Report 2024-25 — 7-10 salesforce per distributor + 9M outlets (https://www.hul.co.in/files/hul-integrated-annual-report-2024-25.pdf)
Salary: Salarite ₹4-5L field sales, Glassdoor ₹4.1L merchandiser, SalaryExpert ₹5.5L visual merchandiser

reconciliation_contract.rule: Per-node Core SAMs MUST sum exactly to sector Core SAM (by construction, not scaling). If the sum does not reconcile, the bottom-up derivation is broken — fix the derivation, do not scale.
reconciliation_contract.current_state_2026_04_22: Auto: node sum $649.5M vs target $650M (Δ -0.5 rounding). FMCG: node sum $208.6M vs target $209M (Δ -0.4 rounding). Reconciled ✓

name_mapping.note: Role library (03_Workflow_Role_Mapping_Sequential.xlsx) uses slightly different workflow block names than HTML AUTO_NODES/FMCG_NODES. Name drift resolved via explicit mapping — preserved here for future sessions.

fmcg_html_to_xlsx.Sales Leadership + Key Accounts & Channe: Sales Leadership & Channels (xlsx 1 block → 2 HTML nodes, split 33/67)
fmcg_html_to_xlsx.S&OP / Demand Planning + Supply Planning: S&OP / Demand Planning (xlsx 1 block → 2 HTML nodes, split 50/50)

data_artifacts.company_universe_csvs: /Users/rohitsar/Documents/Personal/AI Work/Thesis/company_universe/ (4 CSVs: auto/fmcg × Core SAM/Extended SAM, 70 verified firms)

DEPRECATED_NOTE_BRSR_REBUILD.status: Superseded 2026-04-23 by research-refined Extended SAM + Ecosystem FMCG reconciliation
DEPRECATED_NOTE_BRSR_REBUILD.prior_methodology: 4-step chain — total BRSR KW × salary × eligibility × AI rate × discount. Did not narrow KW to selected workflow nodes. Implicitly treated all brand-owner KW as selected.
DEPRECATED_NOTE_BRSR_REBUILD.why_superseded: All KW in all workflows were being counted against TAM, including backlog-node KW (Paint Shop, R&D formulation, Executive Leadership, etc.) which sit outside thesis scope. Step 3 narrows to the 11 Auto + 17 FMCG selected nodes only.

tam_summary.key_innovation_2026_04_23: 3-SAM research refinement: Extended density reanchored via agent research (ACMA member count + Screener distribution + Annual Report scaling); Ecosystem FMCG reconciled to honest $278M = 500K × $6K × 58% × 32% × 50% (was $324M = top-down allocation mismatch). NODE_TAM_CHAIN set to Core-only basis ($377M Auto + $123M FMCG sum) because per-node firm-size × department data not publicly available.
tam_summary.rebuild_note: 2026-04-23: Rebuilt with node-specific salary/elig/AI rate + bottom-up selected share from 100% library coverage

DEPRECATED_NOTE_BRSR_REBUILD.reason: TAM methodology rebuilt from sector-wide NCO 1-5 crosswalk + enterprise fraction to brand-owner BRSR-anchored bottom-up
primary_sources: 17 BRSR filings directly parsed (9 Auto + 8 FMCG)
BRSR Section A 20(a) Employees vs Workers
BRSR Section C Principle 5 E3 median pay
WEF Future of Jobs 2025 + McKinsey MGI GenAI 2023 + Frey-Osborne 2013 (per-role automation)
DEPRECATED_NOTE_BRSR_REBUILD.see: ai_thesis.html Research Methodology Appendix section for full audit trail

node_tam_chain.note: KW column is Core-only (121K × 50.5% Auto, 100K × 60.7% FMCG). TAM formula: kw × sal × elig × ai × 0.5. No core_share multiplier — kw is already Core-reduced.

DEPRECATED_NOTE_NODE_SPECIFIC_REBUILD: 2026-04-23: Chain rebuilt with per-node salary/elig/AI rate. Previous Core-SAM used sector-flat factors. See TAM_MODEL_2026-04-23_v2.json for full derivation.
